reforms.description: The German government is planning a substantial revision of the labour code to promote active job search and improve labour-market performance, which includes measures to facilitate access to risk capital for small and medium-sized enterprises. Additionally, initiatives to enhance the creation and diffusion of technological know-how, including taxation and legal changes to foster venture capital markets, are emphasized to nurture an entrepreneurial climate.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating access to risk capital and enhancing technological know-how are expected to stimulate entrepreneurship and innovation, leading to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform is planned and has been officially announced.
reforms.importance_rationale: The substantial revision of the labour code and the focus on risk capital access represent a significant structural change in support of startups.
reforms.source_quote: Taxation and legal changes need to be made in order to foster the development of venture capital markets.

reforms.description: In 1995, the German government initiated a series of policy initiatives aimed at enhancing government support for research and improving cooperation between the public and private sectors, particularly focusing on small and medium-sized enterprises.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing government support for research and fostering cooperation between sectors is expected to lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The policy initiatives were initiated and are in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiatives represent a significant but narrower change in public funding and support for R&D.
reforms.source_quote: In the area of science and technology, policy initiatives in 1995 have aimed to enhance government support for research and improve cooperation between the public and private sectors.

reforms.description: The government proposed a medium-term programme to reduce the budget deficit and lower public expenditures to pre-unification levels in relation to GDP by the year 2000, aiming for a significant fiscal consolidation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The programme aims to improve fiscal health, which can lead to a more stable economic environment conducive to growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been proposed but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in fiscal policy aimed at long-term economic stability.
reforms.source_quote: The government has proposed a medium-term programme to reduce the budget deficit and lower public expenditures to pre-unification levels in relation to GDP by the year 2000.

reforms.description: The government is considering reforms to lower legal and tax barriers to the development of private pension funds, which is essential for securing the public pension system in the long run.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Lowering barriers to private pension funds can enhance the sustainability of the public pension system, contributing to long-term economic stability.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform is under consideration and has not yet been legislated.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant but does not represent a major overhaul of the pension system.
reforms.source_quote: The legal and tax barriers to the development of private pension funds need to be lowered.
